startingHypothesis.statement: Trust in AI systems is becoming an evidence problem: durable trust will depend less on stated intent and more on independently repeatable assurance signals across evaluation, monitoring, and operational control.
startingHypothesis.rationale: Multiple fresh sources point toward operational evidence, evaluation, and monitoring as core trust mechanisms.

sourcesRead.title: Sparse Feature Geometry of Transformer Circuits for Model Editing, Alignment and Behavioural Control
sourcesRead.summary: Abstract Sparse feature geometry has become a decisive frontier for interpreting, editing, aligning and controlling transformer systems because it treats behaviour not as a monolithic property of a model, but as an emergent consequence of distributed, partially sparse and context-sensitive circuits. This manuscript develops a doctoral-level framework for studying transformer circuits through feature spaces, attribution structures, causal interventions, predictive trustworthiness models, reproducible engineering pip

sourcesRead.title: AI-driven personalization of food systems: From precision nutrition to climate-resilient supply chains
sourcesRead.summary: Traditional large-scale production and standardized interventions cannot address the risks due to individual metabolic diversity, climate change, and supply chain disruptions. This article reviews how artificial intelligence (AI) technology synergistically enhances precision nutrition interventions and climate-resilient supply chain resilience through cross-scale data integration and algorithm optimization, breaking away from the fragmented analytical approach that traditionally separates nutrition science from sup

notes.claim: Sparse Feature Geometry of Transformer Circuits for Model Editing, Alignment and Behavioural Control adds evidence on assurance measurement.
notes.implication: The trust question should be treated as a testable evidence pipeline, not a static policy claim.
notes.limitation: The metadata-only pass should be followed by closer reading before major confidence changes.

notes.claim: AI-driven personalization of food systems: From precision nutrition to climate-resilient supply chains adds evidence on assurance measurement.
notes.implication: The trust question should be treated as a testable evidence pipeline, not a static policy claim.
notes.limitation: The metadata-only pass should be followed by closer reading before major confidence changes.

synthesis: Tonight's open-source scan found 2 relevant artifacts clustered around sparse, feature, transformer, circuits. The working hypothesis remains viable, but the evidence base is still stronger on evaluation and tooling than on independent, repeatable assurance in deployed systems.

hypothesisUpdates.rationale: Multiple fresh sources point toward operational evidence, evaluation, and monitoring as core trust mechanisms.

newHypotheses.statement: Independent AI assurance will converge around continuously refreshed evidence bundles rather than one-time model reports.
Agentic AI trust failures will be easier to detect through workflow telemetry than through benchmark scores alone.

nextSearchPlan: continuous assurance evidence bundles AI systems
agent workflow telemetry trust monitoring evaluation
independent AI audit benchmark operational assurance
unresolvedQuestions: Which assurance signals remain predictive after deployment conditions drift?
What evidence should be independently reproducible versus vendor-attested?
How should agentic workflows expose trust-relevant telemetry without leaking sensitive data?
